objective: Use strategies to compare fractions in real-world problems

pretest.question: Tom ate 2/4 of a pizza. Sara ate 3/4 of the same pizza. Who ate more?
pretest.hint: The pizzas are the same size. Compare the numerators.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.9 Lesson 9.1 'Problem Solving: Compare Fractions' pp.353-356
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 5 Topic F — Comparison, Order, and Size of Fractions
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

pretest.question: Compare: 1/3 ○ 1/6
pretest.hint: When numerators are the same, the smaller denominator means a bigger piece.

pretest.question: Mia painted 2/6 of a wall. Joe painted 2/3 of the same wall. Who painted more?
pretest.hint: Same numerator — which denominator makes bigger pieces?

pretest.question: Compare: 4/6 ○ 5/6
pretest.hint: Same denominator. Which numerator is larger?

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.9 Lesson 9.1 'Problem Solving: Compare Fractions' pp.353-356
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 5 Topic F — Comparison, Order, and Size of Fractions

learn.title: 같은 분모 비교 — 분자가 큰 쪽이 큼
learn.content: When two fractions have the SAME DENOMINATOR, the pieces are the same size. Just compare how many pieces: the bigger numerator is the greater fraction. Example: 3/8 < 5/8 because 3 pieces < 5 pieces. 예: Which is greater: 2/6 or 4/6? → 4/6. (Same denominator (6). 4 pieces > 2 pieces, so 4/6 > 2/6.)

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.9 Lesson 9.1 'Problem Solving: Compare Fractions' pp.353-356
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 5 Topic F — Comparison, Order, and Size of Fractions
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: 같은 분자 비교 — 분모가 작은 쪽이 큼
learn.content: When two fractions have the SAME NUMERATOR, you have the same number of pieces — but the pieces are different sizes. A smaller denominator means bigger pieces. Example: 2/3 > 2/8 because thirds are bigger than eighths. 예: Which is greater: 1/4 or 1/2? → 1/2. (Same numerator (1). Halves are bigger than fourths, so 1/2 > 1/4.)

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.9 Lesson 9.1 'Problem Solving: Compare Fractions' pp.353-356
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 5 Topic F — Comparison, Order, and Size of Fractions
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: 같은 전체 가정 — 비교의 전제
learn.content: In word problems, first find the two fractions, then decide: same denominator? Compare numerators. Same numerator? Compare denominators. Use >, <, or = to show the result. 예: Amy ran 3/4 of a lap. Ben ran 3/6 of the same lap. Who ran farther? → Amy. (Same numerator (3). Fourths are bigger than sixths, so 3/4 > 3/6. Amy ran farther.)

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.9 Lesson 9.1 'Problem Solving: Compare Fractions' pp.353-356
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 5 Topic F — Comparison, Order, and Size of Fractions
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: 전략 선택 — 분모/분자 무엇이 같나?
learn.content: 두 분수를 비교할 때 먼저 확인:   · 분모가 같다 → 분자가 큰 쪽이 큰 분수 (3/8 < 5/8).   · 분자가 같다 → 분모가 작은 쪽이 큰 분수 (3/4 > 3/6).   · 둘 다 다르다 → 1/2 같은 기준 분수와 비교 (벤치마크). 🔍 1단계: 같은 부분 찾기. 2단계: 규칙 적용.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.9 Lesson 9.1 'Problem Solving: Compare Fractions' pp.353-356
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 5 Topic F — Comparison, Order, and Size of Fractions
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.9 Lesson 9.1 'Problem Solving: Compare Fractions' pp.353-356
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 5 Topic F — Comparison, Order, and Size of Fractions
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: 분수 비교 3단계 루틴
learn.content: 두 분수 비교 3단계:   단계 1) 같은 전체인지 확인 (같은 피자, 같은 막대).   단계 2) 분모/분자 중 같은 부분 찾기 → 규칙 선택.   단계 3) 규칙 적용해 부등호 결정. 예: 2/6 vs 4/6 → 1) 같은 피자 ✓, 2) 같은 분모, 3) 4>2이므로 2/6 < 4/6.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.9 Lesson 9.1 'Problem Solving: Compare Fractions' pp.353-356
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 5 Topic F — Comparison, Order, and Size of Fractions
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: 분모 클수록 크다? — 자주 하는 실수
learn.content: 흔한 실수 (M02): 분모가 큰 쪽이 더 크다고 착각. 예: 1/8 vs 1/3 → '8 > 3이니까 1/8이 더 큼'? ❌ 사실: 분모가 클수록 한 조각은 더 작음. 1/8 < 1/3. 🔍 직관: 케이크 8조각 < 케이크 3조각 (한 조각의 크기). 교정: 분자가 같으면 분모 작은 쪽이 큼.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.9 Lesson 9.1 'Problem Solving: Compare Fractions' pp.353-356
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 5 Topic F — Comparison, Order, and Size of Fractions
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: 문장제에서 비교 단계
learn.content: 문장제에서 분수 비교 단계:   단계 1) 같은 전체인지 확인 (같은 길이 막대, 같은 크기 피자).   단계 2) 두 분수 추출.   단계 3) 비교 → 누가/어느 쪽이 더 많이/적게? 예: 'Tom 3/8 피자, Sara 5/8 피자. 누가 더?' → 3/8 < 5/8 → Sara. 흔한 실수: 다른 크기 비교 — '같은 전체' 조건을 놓침.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.9 Lesson 9.1 'Problem Solving: Compare Fractions' pp.353-356
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 5 Topic F — Comparison, Order, and Size of Fractions
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

try.question: Two gardens are the same size. Rosa planted flowers in 2/3 of her garden. Tom planted flowers in 2/8 of his garden. Who planted more? Compare 2/3 and 2/8.
try.hint: Same numerator. Which denominator means bigger pieces?
guided_steps: Step 3: Thirds are bigger than eighths, so 2/3 > 2/8

practice_r3.question: Is 3/4 greater than, less than, or equal to 3/6? Explain how you know WITHOUT drawing a picture.
practice_r3.answer: 3/4 > 3/6. Same numerator (3). Fourths are bigger pieces than sixths because the whole is split into fewer parts. So 3 bigger pieces > 3 smaller pieces.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.9 Lesson 9.1 'Problem Solving: Compare Fractions' pp.353-356
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 5 Topic F — Comparison, Order, and Size of Fractions
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

practice_r3.question: Maya says 1/8 is greater than 1/3 because 8 is greater than 3. Is Maya correct? Explain why or why not.
practice_r3.answer: Maya is NOT correct. A bigger denominator means smaller pieces. 1/8 is a smaller piece than 1/3, so 1/8 < 1/3.
